metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tag the 'dogfooded in advisory Gerrit review' Success Criterion with `[operator-attested]` (per ADR 0043) so its verification path (a recorded Gerrit change-id / vote / timestamp) is explicit, or add a corresponding AC line with a deterministic in-session command.
evidence: 'dogfooded in advisory Gerrit review (children 225a, 9447)' — requires a live Gerrit review event external to the repo/CI
ADR 0043 [operator-attested] exception is not applied to this bullet
The AC block contains no corresponding verifiable command for the dogfooding outcome
findings.finding: The Success Criterion 'dogfooded in advisory Gerrit review (children 225a, 9447)' is not tagged `[operator-attested]` but describes an external, post-session action (an actual advisory Gerrit review must occur), making it non-verifiable in-session via repo artifacts or CI.
findings.location: Success Criteria — third bullet ('The `project.review-phase-boundaries` criterion and its balanced eval corpus exist and are dogfooded in advisory Gerrit review')
scenarios: An executor marks the epic done after authoring the criterion and corpus (both repo-verifiable) but before any advisory review has occurred; the SC is technically unmet but unenforceable without the tag
A reviewer cannot confirm the SC in-session and must chase an external Gerrit link
findings.suggested_fix: Prefix the bullet: '[operator-attested] The `project.review-phase-boundaries` criterion and its balanced eval corpus exist and are dogfooded in advisory Gerrit review; attested by Gerrit change-id recorded on child 9447.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that directly verifies the behavioral end-state: e.g. a smoke-test or integration-test invocation confirming a project criterion in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` produces findings with a `project.<name>` id, is gated by Pass 3, and is accepted by the `criteria eval` CLI without error.
evidence: Success Criteria: 'A project code-review criterion activated in .rebar/criteria_routing.json executes once through Round A/B, carries its logical project.<name> id on every finding, has its project threshold/blocking/nit-suppression govern the deterministic Pass 3, resolves through the eval/calibration path, and is admitted by the criteria eval CLI'
AC item 1: 'each seam child (1–6) passes rebar review-plan <child> with no blocking finding'
AC item 2: 'Each root-cause seam point is covered by exactly one seam child with no file overlap'
AC item 3: 'the epic supersedes jira-reb-1001, 2c14, and 8a4b, and all three are closed'
findings.finding: The Success Criteria describe specific behavioral outcomes of the seam fix — project criterion executes once through Round A/B, carries its `project.<name>` id on every finding, has its threshold/blocking/nit-suppression govern Pass 3, resolves through the eval/calibration path, and is admitted by the CLI — but none of the three Acceptance Criteria verify any of these runtime behaviors. The ACs only verify that children exist, pass plan-review, cover the five drop points, and close superseded items. The core behavioral promise has no covering AC.
findings.location: Success Criteria (first bullet) vs. Acceptance Criteria (all three items)
scenarios: All eight children ship and pass plan review, yet the seam silently misbehaves at runtime (e.g. logical id is wrong, Pass 3 uses wrong thresholds) — nothing in the ACs would catch this.
A stakeholder reading the ACs cannot determine whether the seam fix was verified to work end-to-end.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a child or explicit AC to an existing child that creates the three `supersedes` edges (this epic → jira-reb-1001, 2c14, 8a4b) and sets status=closed on each, verified by `rebar show` commands.
evidence: Parent AC: 'The epic supersedes jira-reb-1001, 2c14, and 8a4b, and all three are closed; verified by `rebar show 119f-63e3-34b7-4f96` listing three `supersedes` edges and `rebar show <each>` reporting `status=closed`.'
Roster cross-check: children 19dd, 225a, 41e2, 58ef, 761e, 8153, 9447, fc72 — none has a scope item or AC that creates supersedes edges or sets status=closed on those three tickets.
Children reference 2c14/8a4b only as context ('superseded bug 8a4b', 'superseding jira-reb-1001') or as live dependencies, not as targets for status change.
findings.finding: No child owns the work of creating `supersedes` edges or setting status=closed on jira-reb-1001, story 2c14, and bug 8a4b. The parent's acceptance criterion AC-C ('The epic supersedes jira-reb-1001, 2c14, and 8a4b, and all three are closed; verified by `rebar show 119f...` listing three `supersedes` edges and `rebar show <each>` reporting status=closed') requires a concrete action that no child's scope or AC covers.
findings.location: Parent AC-C; no child in the sibling roster
scenarios: The eight children all land green, but `rebar show 119f-63e3-34b7-4f96` does not list three supersedes edges and `rebar show jira-reb-1001` still shows status=open — AC-C fails the completion verifier.
The parent's success criterion 'jira-reb-1001, 2c14, and 8a4b are superseded by this epic and closed' is never satisfied.
findings.suggested_fix: Either assign the closure/supersession action to child 19dd (the docs/process child at the end of the seam stack) or create a ninth 'close-out' child that explicitly performs `rebar supersede` and `rebar close` for all three tickets and verifies the resulting state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies parity: e.g. 'the existing test suite for built-in overlays passes unchanged, and a canary run on an overlay-absent repo produces output byte-identical to pre-epic baseline; verified by CI green on the unmodified built-in overlay test suite.'
evidence: Success Criteria: 'An overlay-absent repo behaves byte-identically to today; the 13 built-in overlays are unchanged (parity).'
No AC references parity, byte-identical behavior, regression testing, or the 13 built-in overlays.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria guarantees 'An overlay-absent repo behaves byte-identically to today; the 13 built-in overlays are unchanged (parity)' but no Acceptance Criterion verifies this parity guarantee. This is a described deliverable (regression safety) with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Success Criteria (second bullet) vs. Acceptance Criteria (all three items)
scenarios: A seam change inadvertently alters behavior for repos without a project overlay or changes a built-in overlay; nothing in the ACs would detect this regression.

evidence: docs/llm-framework.md: 19dd-7d89-972b-4274 & 225a-323c-a9f6-436b (no ordering edge)
tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py: 41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847 & 58ef-91fa-20c1-4f29 (no ordering edge)
tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py: 41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847 & 761e-5af2-ed57-408a (no ordering edge)
tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py: 41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847 & fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 (no ordering edge)
tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py: 58ef-91fa-20c1-4f29 & 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 (no ordering edge)
tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py: 58ef-91fa-20c1-4f29 & fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 (no ordering edge)
tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py: 761e-5af2-ed57-408a & 8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 (no ordering edge)
findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: etc.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether child 6 (docs) is exempt from the RED→GREEN test-slice rule, and update the Decomposition to say 'each code-bearing seam child (1–5) grows one RED→GREEN slice' or add a lightweight contract-verification test for the docs child.
evidence: 'each single-concern on a disjoint production-file set, each growing one RED→GREEN slice of the shared test file tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py'
child 6: 'Docs contract (docs/llm-framework.md) — depends on 5.'
findings.finding: The Decomposition states 'each [seam child] growing one RED→GREEN slice of the shared test file `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py`', but child 6 (Docs contract, `docs/llm-framework.md`) is a documentation-only change that cannot grow a test slice for a test file about project criteria runtime behavior. This is a contradiction between the stated incremental-green testing strategy and the actual composition of the seam stack.
findings.location: Decomposition section — 'Eight children. The six seam children land as an ordered Gerrit stack … each growing one RED→GREEN slice of the shared test file' vs. child 6 entry 'Docs contract (docs/llm-framework.md)'
scenarios: An implementer following the plan literally will try to write a RED→GREEN test slice for a docs-only change, wasting time or producing a vacuous test.
A reviewer checking that child 6 grows a test slice will find no meaningful slice and flag it as non-conformant.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Decomposition sentence to: 'each code-bearing seam child (1–5) grows one RED→GREEN slice of the shared test file `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py`; child 6 (docs) requires no new test slice.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update children 225a and 9447's Dependency sections to reference the concrete seam children within this epic (e.g., 41e2 for the eval/CLI surface) instead of the superseded tickets 2c14 and 8a4b.
evidence: child 225a Dependency: 'Depends on the linked integration-seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`, whose child bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` owns the concrete repair.'

child 9447 Dependencies: 'Depends on both the generic integration-seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` (including its child bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8`) and the project-criterion authoring story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b`.'

Parent context: 'superseding jira-reb-1001 (the prior umbrella), story 2c14 (the generic-seam story), and bug 8a4b (the seam defect)' — these are presented as superseded, with the actual repair work decomposed into this epic's seam children.
The actual seam repair is performed by children fc72, 761e, 58ef, 8153, 41e2 within this epic — not by 2c14/8a4b.
findings.finding: Children 225a and 9447 list superseded ticket `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` and its child bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` as live `Dependency` entries, but the parent states these are superseded by this epic and should be closed. If they are closed/superseded, these dependency edges are unresolvable at execution time.
findings.location: child 225a-323c-a9f6-436b Dependency section; child 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d Dependencies section
scenarios: A dependency-tracker tool blocks child 225a or 9447 from being marked ready because their listed dependency 2c14 is in status=closed/superseded, treating it as an unresolvable blocker.
An engineer reading child 9447 follows the 2c14/8a4b dependency link and finds closed/superseded tickets with no actionable work, creating confusion about what prerequisite actually needs to be done.

The intent (depend on the seam repair) is lost: the correct dependencies for 225a and 9447 are on children fc72, 761e, 58ef, 8153, 41e2 (or shorthand 'depends on seam child 5, 41e2'), not on tickets being closed out.
findings.suggested_fix: In child 225a: replace 'Depends on the linked integration-seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`, whose child bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` owns the concrete repair' with 'Depends on the seam children of this epic, specifically child 41e2 (eval/CLI surface), which must land before this story.' In child 9447: replace the 2c14/8a4b dependency with 'Depends on seam children fc72, 761e, 58ef, 8153, 41e2 (the working seam) and child 225a (criterion authorship).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify 'both gates' by naming the two contexts explicitly (e.g., 'when a project id is registered in both the code-review overlay and the plan-review overlay simultaneously') in the Success Criteria first bullet.
evidence: 'rejected as ambiguous only when active in both gates' — 'gates' is used without a referent in this sentence
The plan uses 'gate' to mean different things: Round A/B, Pass 3, and the plan-review vs code-review top-level gate distinction — none are labeled 'both gates' in one place
findings.finding: 'Rejected as ambiguous only when active in both gates' in the CLI-admission Success Criterion does not define what 'both gates' refers to, leaving the executor to infer which two execution contexts constitute a conflict.
findings.location: Success Criteria — first bullet ('…admitted by the `criteria eval` CLI (rejected as ambiguous only when active in both gates)')
scenarios: An executor implementing child 5 (CLI admission, `41e2`) interprets 'both gates' as Round A and Round B within code-review rather than as the code-review vs plan-review registry distinction, producing the wrong rejection predicate
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(rejected as ambiguous only when active in both gates)' with '(rejected as ambiguous only when the same project criterion id is simultaneously active in both the code-review and plan-review overlays)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit dependency from child 225a on child 19dd (or vice versa, with a clear ordering rule) so the two docs edits land in a defined sequence without conflict.
evidence: child 19dd Scope: '`docs/llm-framework.md` — add the "Code-review project criteria" contract section.'
child 225a Scope: 'Add a `Project dogfood: review-phase-boundaries` subsection to `docs/llm-framework.md` explaining the project-owned placement, phase contract, advisory posture, and eval corpus.'
Parent decomposition: 'No two seam children touch the same production file' — this guarantee is stated only for seam children; feature children 7–8 are not bound by it.
child 225a's Dependency section: 'Depends on the linked integration-seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`...' — does not list child 19dd as a dependency despite modifying the same file.
findings.finding: Children 19dd (seam child 6) and 225a (feature child 7) both modify `docs/llm-framework.md` with no declared ordering dependency between them, creating a scope overlap outside the parent's disjoint-file guarantee (which applies only to seam children 1–6).
scenarios: child 19dd and child 225a are reviewed and merged in parallel or out of order, causing merge conflicts or overwritten content in docs/llm-framework.md.

child 225a's `Project dogfood` subsection lands before child 19dd's `Code-review project criteria` section, producing a document where the dogfood subsection appears without the parent contract section it references.

The docs acceptance check in child 19dd passes, but child 225a's later edit inadvertently removes or overwrites one of the required contract strings ('round_a', 'effective routing', etc.), silently breaking child 19dd's verified AC.
findings.suggested_fix: In child 225a's Dependency section, add: 'Also depends on child 19dd (docs contract) since both modify docs/llm-framework.md; 225a must land after 19dd to avoid merge conflicts and to reference the established contract section.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `_validate_routing_entry` in `overlay.py` to produce a gate-aware error message (either pass `gate_key` into the validator, or make the error message generic, e.g. `'the rubric must be filesystem-safe'`), so the path cited is correct for both `plan_review` and `code_review` gates; add this to child fc72's scope since it is the foundation child whose gate-aware prefix map is the authority.
evidence: overlay.py:149: `"stored at plan-review-project-<name>.md, so the name must be filesystem-safe"` — hardcodes the plan-review prefix in a gate-shared validator
overlay.py:140-141: the embedded comment also reads `criterion_prompt_id (project.<name> → plan-review-project-<name>)` — only the plan-review mapping is described
Child fc72 (fc72-bf7e-7652-4228) Scope: only `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` and `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` — overlay.py is absent
All six seam children (fc72/761e/58ef/8153/41e2/19dd) file_impact lists do not include `overlay.py`

After fc72 lands, `criterion_prompt_id('project.foo', gate_key='code_review')` → `'code-review-project-foo'`, so the rubric path is `code-review-project-<name>.md`, not the string in the error message
findings.finding: The shared `_validate_routing_entry` validator in `overlay.py` hardcodes the plan-review rubric path in its error message (`stored at plan-review-project-<name>.md`), but this validator is called for BOTH gates. After child `fc72` makes `criterion_prompt_id` gate-aware (so a code-review project criterion's rubric lives at `code-review-project-<name>.md`), this error message will actively misdirect a code-review criterion author. No seam child's declared file-impact list includes `overlay.py`, so the inconsistency is introduced by the epic and not corrected by it.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:146-149 (the `_validate_routing_entry` error message); none of the seam children (fc72, 761e, 58ef, 8153, 41e2, 19dd) list overlay.py in their ## Scope or file_impact
scenarios: A developer adds a project code-review criterion with an invalid name (e.g. containing underscore); the CriteriaError tells them the rubric must be at `plan-review-project-<name>.md`, which is wrong for the code-review gate — they look in the wrong prompt namespace and cannot self-diagnose the issue.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_validate_routing_entry`, replace the hardcoded path literal with a generic message: `"the criterion name must be filesystem-safe (alnum + dash) so it can form a valid prompt-library file id"`. Alternatively, pass an optional `gate_key` parameter and derive the correct prefix via the new `_PROMPT_PREFIX` map from fc72.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the minimum corpus definition for 'balanced eval corpus' in the Success Criteria or child 225a's scope (e.g., minimum N examples, expected pass/fail ratio, one example per severity bucket).
evidence: 'balanced eval corpus' — 'balanced' is a subjective term with no stated count, ratio, or coverage rule
No other section of the plan defines the corpus shape or acceptance threshold for the eval set
findings.finding: 'Balanced eval corpus' in the Success Criteria and referenced in child 225a's scope has no definition of what 'balanced' means (example count, true-positive/true-negative ratio, severity distribution), making it an implicit acceptance criterion an executor cannot objectively satisfy.
findings.location: Success Criteria — third bullet ('…its balanced eval corpus exist…'); also Decomposition child 225a ('Author `project.review-phase-boundaries` + eval corpus')
scenarios: The executor of child 225a authors a corpus of 2 positive + 2 negative examples and considers it 'balanced'; a reviewer disagrees; no arbiter exists
The Success Criterion cannot be checked in-session because 'balanced' has no deterministic definition

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (or expand-contract sequencing) for the six-child seam stack, describing how to revert or isolate a mid-stack regression without breaking the already-merged children's built-in parity guarantee.
evidence: 'An overlay-absent repo behaves byte-identically to today; the 13 built-in overlays are unchanged (parity).'

Six production files changed across six Gerrit children (ids.py, batch_runner.py, gate_dispatch.py, workflow_ops.py, eval_solver.py, eval.py, _llm_eval_commands.py) with no stated revert/rollback procedure.
ABSENCE: No expand-contract sequencing described (e.g., add new path first, cut over, remove old path); no back-out commit or flag described.
findings.finding: The plan asserts byte-identical parity for overlay-absent repos and unchanged built-in overlays, but provides no rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequencing if a mid-stack seam child regresses existing behavior across the six touched production files.
findings.location: Success Criteria (parity statement); Decomposition (seam stack children 1–6)
scenarios: A seam child (e.g., child 3, Pass-3 routing) silently changes routing for a built-in criterion in an overlay-absent repo; the Gerrit stack is already partially merged and there is no documented path to revert just that child without unwinding the dependents.
Child 2's fan-in change alters the order or deduplication of Round A/B results for existing built-in criteria, causing parity regression with no rollback guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: For each seam child, add a short 'Rollback' subsection: state which prior symbol/behaviour the child replaces, that the old code path is removed in this child, and that a revert of just this child restores the prior behaviour. Alternatively, sequence child 1 as an additive new symbol (new function alongside old), children 2–5 as cut-overs, and a cleanup child that removes the old symbol — a classic expand-contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task (or expand child 6's scope) to review ADRs 0015/0016/0017 for stale references and amend or supersede any sections that describe the pre-fix broken state.
evidence: 'registry story 5065; ADRs 0015/0016/0017; contract in docs/llm-framework.md' — these ADRs describe the very promise being fixed.
Child 6 scope is limited to 'docs/llm-framework.md'; no mention of ADR updates.
ABSENCE: No AC line or child task covers reviewing/amending ADRs 0015/0016/0017 post-fix.
findings.finding: ADRs 0015/0016/0017, which advertise the project-criteria extension promise that was previously broken, are not listed for amendment; after the seam is fixed they may contain stale caveats, broken-state descriptions, or implementation notes that no longer reflect reality.
findings.location: Context / Problem (references ADRs 0015/0016/0017); Success Criteria; Decomposition child 6 (`19dd`, docs/llm-framework.md only)
scenarios: A future developer reads ADR 0015 and finds a note that 'project code-review criteria are not yet threaded through Round A/B' — an accurate statement pre-fix but stale post-fix — and incorrectly concludes the feature is still broken.
ADR 0016/0017 reference the seam's implementation details; after ids.py and batch_runner.py are changed those references point to obsolete line numbers or removed symbols.
findings.suggested_fix: In child 6's scope, explicitly list 'review ADRs 0015, 0016, 0017 for stale content; add an amendment note or supersede section in each ADR that described the broken seam, pointing to the new docs/llm-framework.md contract.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in child 6's scope whether ADRs 0015/0016/0017 will be amended (or a new ADR created) to cover the `project.<name>` id namespace extension, or explicitly justify why llm-framework.md alone is the authoritative record.
evidence: Plan references 'ADRs 0015/0016/0017' as existing context for the criteria routing design.
Child 6 scope is only 'docs/llm-framework.md' — no mention of ADR amendment or new ADR.
The new `project.<name>` id namespace and its threading through five runtime seam points constitutes a novel architectural decision extending ADRs 0015/0016/0017's scope.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural extension to the criterion-id namespace (`project.<name>`) and threads it through five runtime points, but does not specify whether existing ADRs 0015/0016/0017 (which govern criteria routing) will be amended or a new ADR created — child 6 only mandates a `docs/llm-framework.md` update.
findings.location: Decomposition — child 6 (`19dd`): 'Docs contract (`docs/llm-framework.md`)'; Success Criteria — 'The runtime contract is documented in `docs/llm-framework.md`'
scenarios: A future contributor consults ADR 0015/0016/0017 for the authoritative criteria routing contract and finds the project-extension mechanism undocumented there, leading to drift or re-implementation of the seam.
An auditor checking architectural decisions finds llm-framework.md updated but ADRs silent, creating an inconsistent source of truth.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 6's scope: 'Update or append to ADR-0015 (or whichever ADR governs criterion id assignment) a section describing the `project.<name>` namespace, how it is resolved, and its runtime contract.' If the ADRs are intentionally immutable, note in the plan that llm-framework.md supersedes them for this extension.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
